title: abutilon indicum subsp. guineense
intro: Abutilon indicum subsp. guineense, commonly known as the Abutilon or Indian mallow, is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family and is native to Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Abutilon indicum subsp. guineense include reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
